q.en: About how many different species of owls live around the world?
q.zh: 世界上大约生活着多少种不同的猫头鹰？

options.en: Exactly 100
About 50
More than 200
Fewer than 10 according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 不到10种（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states owls are 'one of more than two hundred different species of owls that live around the world.'
explain.zh: 书中写道，猫头鹰是'世界上生活的200多种不同猫头鹰中的一种'。

q.en: On which continent do owls NOT live?
q.zh: 猫头鹰不生活在哪个大洲？

options.en: Antarctica
Asia
Europe according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 欧洲（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Africa

explain.en: The text says owls live on 'every continent except Antarctica.'
explain.zh: 文中说猫头鹰生活在'除南极洲以外的每一个大洲'。

q.en: How far can an owl rotate its head?
q.zh: 猫头鹰能把头转动多少度？

options.en: A full 360 degrees
About 180 degrees
About 90 degrees
About 280 degrees

explain.en: Owls can 'rotate their heads almost all the way around—280 degrees.'
explain.zh: 猫头鹰能够'把头几乎转到一整圈——280度'。

q.en: In the glossary, what does the word 'rotate' mean?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，'rotate'（转动）这个词是什么意思？

options.en: To stay alive
To turn on a center; to revolve
To act harmfully toward
A group of similar living things according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一群相似的生物（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'rotate (v.)' as 'to turn on a center; to revolve.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将'rotate（动词）'定义为'绕中心转动；旋转'。

q.en: At the end of the book, the author writes that if you stay out late, people might think you're a 'night owl.' What does 'night owl' mean here?
q.zh: 在书的结尾，作者写道如果你在外面待到很晚，人们可能会认为你是一只'夜猫子'（night owl）。这里的'night owl'是什么意思？

options.en: A person who stays up or is active late at night
options.zh: 熬夜或深夜仍活跃的人

options.en: A kind of owl that only lives in barns according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一种只生活在谷仓里的猫头鹰（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: An owl that cannot fly
A baby owl

explain.en: The playful ending compares a person staying out late to a 'night owl,' meaning someone active at night.
explain.zh: 这个俏皮的结尾把在外面待到很晚的人比作'夜猫子'，指深夜仍活跃的人。

q.en: According to the book, why do MOST owls hunt at night?
q.zh: 根据这本书，大多数猫头鹰为什么在夜间捕猎？

options.en: To avoid competing with daytime hunters such as eagles, hawks, and falcons
options.zh: 为了不与鹰、隼、猎鹰等白天捕猎者竞争

options.en: Because they live only inside caves
options.zh: 因为它们只生活在洞穴里

options.en: Because their ears stop working during the day according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为它们的耳朵在白天就失灵了（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Because they cannot open their eyes in daylight
options.zh: 因为它们在白天睁不开眼睛

explain.en: The book explains that because most owls hunt at night, 'they don't have to compete with daytime hunters such as eagles, hawks, and falcons.'
explain.zh: 书中解释说，因为大多数猫头鹰在夜间捕猎，所以'它们不必与鹰、隼、猎鹰等白天捕猎者竞争'。

q.en: Owls have flat, round faces that funnel sound to their ears. How does this help them?
q.zh: 猫头鹰有扁平、圆圆的脸，能把声音汇聚到耳朵里。这对它们有什么帮助？

options.en: It helps them chew food according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 帮助它们咀嚼食物（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: It helps them locate prey by sound
options.zh: 帮助它们通过声音定位猎物

options.en: It keeps them warm
It scares away enemies

explain.en: The text says owls move the feathers around their faces to send more sound to one ear or the other, and 'Doing this helps them locate prey.'
explain.zh: 文中说猫头鹰移动脸周围的羽毛，把更多声音送进一只或另一只耳朵，而'这样做能帮助它们定位猎物'。

q.en: The author explains that scientists look through owl pellets. Why does the author include this detail?
q.zh: 作者解释了科学家会仔细查看猫头鹰的食丸。作者为什么要加入这个细节？

options.en: To describe the colors of owl feathers
options.zh: 为了描述猫头鹰羽毛的颜色

options.en: To show how people learn what owls eat
options.zh: 为了说明人们如何了解猫头鹰吃什么

options.en: To prove that owls are dangerous to people
options.zh: 为了证明猫头鹰对人类很危险

options.en: To explain how owls learn to fly
options.zh: 为了解释猫头鹰如何学会飞翔

explain.en: The book says 'Scientists look through the pellets to learn what owls eat,' so the detail shows how people study owls' diet.
explain.zh: 书中说'科学家仔细查看食丸，以了解猫头鹰吃什么'，所以这个细节说明了人们如何研究猫头鹰的食谱。

q.en: The author tells the story of a great horned owl in Florida tackling a 6-foot indigo snake. What is the author's purpose?
q.zh: 作者讲述了佛罗里达一只大角鸮与一条6英尺长的靛蓝蛇搏斗的故事。作者的写作意图是什么？

options.en: To describe the weather in Florida according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了描述佛罗里达的天气（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To explain how snakes are born
options.zh: 为了解释蛇是如何出生的

options.en: To teach about cutting down trees
options.zh: 为了讲授砍伐树木的知识

options.en: To show that owls are skillful and powerful hunters
options.zh: 为了说明猫头鹰是技术高超且强壮的捕猎者

explain.en: The text calls the great horned owl 'a skillful hunter' and gives the snake story as an example of its hunting ability.
explain.zh: 文中称大角鸮为'技术高超的猎手'，并把这个蛇的故事作为它捕猎能力的例子。

q.en: About how many mice may a barn owl eat in its lifetime?
q.zh: 一只仓鸮一生大约能吃多少只老鼠？

options.en: 1,400 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The book states, 'In its lifetime, a barn owl may eat 11,000 mice!' (1,400 is the number of voles a great gray owl may eat in a year.)
explain.zh: 书中写道：'一只仓鸮一生可能吃掉11,000只老鼠！'（1,400只是大灰鸮一年可能吃掉的田鼠数量。）

prompt.en: Owls such as the spotted owl depend on special habitats. What could people do to help protect owls and the places they live?
prompt.zh: 像斑点猫头鹰这样的猫头鹰依赖特殊的栖息地。人们可以做些什么来帮助保护猫头鹰和它们生活的地方？

en: The book notes that by 1994, logging had destroyed 80 percent of the spotted owl's old-growth forest habitat; protecting old forests helps.
Some owls, like burrowing owls, need open spaces (prairies, golf courses) and safe places to nest underground.
Owls help control pests such as mice and voles, so they are important to the food chain and to people.
zh: 书中提到，到1994年，伐木已经摧毁了斑点猫头鹰80%的原始森林栖息地；保护古老森林会有所帮助。
有些猫头鹰（如穴居猫头鹰）需要开阔地带（草原、高尔夫球场）以及安全的地下筑巢处。
猫头鹰帮助控制老鼠和田鼠等有害动物，因此它们对食物链和人类都很重要。
